summary: Integrated ADR 0306 onto latest `origin/main` baseline `2411a7cd428e0eba17168aa5eed66f04c4ed48dd` as repository version `0.177.119` while preserving the already-current platform baseline `0.130.77`. The earlier exact-main private Gitea proof derived from source commit `ef2803b3830cf05bb22128dcfa7860f9002b75b0` remains the first hosted verification boundary on platform version `0.130.75`; this final integration cut adds release-manager evidence, rebased build-server validation, detached exact-head `remote-validate` success, and a bounded `pre-push-gate` caveat tied to transient `registry.lv3.org/check-runner/infra:2026.03.23` `502 Bad Gateway` responses rather than an ADR 0306 regression.

verification.observed: The earlier governed exact-main replay published source commit `ef2803b3830cf05bb22128dcfa7860f9002b75b0` onto the private Gitea `main` snapshot as commit `8d17ace04285790b0eca9de5415b75967cdfecf8` on top of prior private-main head `a10101240780d755f27988ee7352ac4f7cc74a7c`.

verification.check: Exact-main hosted validation and release-bundle paths
verification.observed: Private Gitea `release-bundle.yml` run `178` / jobs `235` and `236`, plus `validate.yml` run `179` / job `237`, all concluded `success` on `main` after the exact-main snapshot publication.

verification.check: Latest-main release cut and rebased branch validation
verification.observed: On top of latest `origin/main` commit `2411a7cd428e0eba17168aa5eed66f04c4ed48dd`, `scripts/release_manager.py status --json`, the patch dry run, and the patch release write all completed and cut repository version `0.177.119`. The rebased branch candidate then passed `make check-build-server`, and `make remote-validate` exercised every blocking ADR 0306 lane; the only remaining non-pass there was the designed terminal-workstream `workstream-surfaces` guard on branch `codex/ws-0306-mainline-final-r3`.

verification.check: Detached exact-head build-server and remote-validate replay
verification.observed: The committed `0.177.119` candidate was replayed from detached `HEAD` commit `56fd6422c43789a68660150baaf0e7d0b0376b99`; both `make check-build-server` and `make remote-validate` passed cleanly there, and `workstream-surfaces` also passed once the branch-specific terminal guard no longer applied.

The detached `make pre-push-gate` wrapper was exercised twice. Both replays passed every remote blocking check except `packer-validate` and `tofu-validate`, which hit transient `502 Bad Gateway` pulls for `registry.lv3.org/check-runner/infra:2026.03.23`. The fallback then proved `packer-validate` and `tofu-validate`, but the wrapper stayed non-green because controller-local fallback reran already-green heavy checks under fixed timeouts.

The remaining fallback-only timeout victims were rechecked directly on the detached exact head: governed `ansible-lint` passed with warnings only, and the direct Trivy filesystem `security-scan` passed with no blocking findings.

verification.check: Build-server session cleanup for final validation capacity
verification.observed: Inactive ADR 0306 build-server session roots were removed only after confirming no active validation process still referenced them, reclaiming bounded scratch space for the final remote replays.

notes: ADR 0306 is now integrated in repository version `0.177.119`, while ADR metadata preserves `Implemented In Platform Version: 0.130.75` as the first live platform version where the control became true.
The detached exact-head `remote-validate` replay passed cleanly after the release cut and re-verified the released `0.177.119` automation surfaces without the branch-only `workstream-surfaces` guard.

The detached exact-head `pre-push-gate` wrapper remained partial only because `registry.lv3.org/check-runner/infra:2026.03.23` returned transient `502 Bad Gateway` responses and the controller fallback reran already-green heavy checks under fixed timeouts; no ADR 0306 functional regression was found in the direct `ansible-lint` and Trivy `security-scan` replays.

A bounded manual cleanup of inactive ADR 0306 build-server session roots was required to recover enough remote scratch capacity for the final replays, and the remote-build-gateway runbook now records the same safety rule.
